title: The Fast-Forward Parliament
tagline: Ten headless agents agree on one value with no server, no leader, and no votes counted — git's fast-forward rule is the only referee.
holy_shit: Distributed consensus with nothing actually running: N agents race to push the same branch, git's atomic ref-update lets exactly one fast-forward win per parent, and that single accepted push IS the decision. Paxos, realized by a remote that has no idea it's a parliament.
powered_by: git's fast-forward-only ref update — a native, atomic compare-and-swap — as the consensus primitive; the live panel realizes the identical rule with the browser's own atomic ref (an IndexedDB read-write transaction) across ten concurrent agents, no server; /track's verified extract cell (fetch -> recompute SHA-256 -> refuse-on-mismatch) supplies the frame they ratify; /resolver names the agreed tip as name@sha8.
agent_scenario: When N autonomous agents must agree on one value (or a canonical order) with no coordinator, no quorum math, and no vote tally: model the decision as an atomic compare-and-swap on one shared ref (a git fast-forward-only push, or a native atomic transaction) — every agent bases on the same parent, exactly one write lands per parent, and the accepted ref IS the decision.
author: kody-w + Claude
